<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A comprehensive educational primer created for the peasant school Tolstoy founded at his Yasnaya Polyana estate. The book combined reading instruction with original fables, folk tales, and moral stories written in simple, direct prose. Tolstoy spent years on it and considered it among his most important works, aiming to give Russian peasant children literature worthy of them. A revised and expanded version, the *New Azbuka*, followed in 1875.
